perturbation.original_text: In consideration of receiving Confidential Informat ion from the City of Winnipeg and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, ___________________________(insert name of legal name of counterparty) (the “Confidant”) agrees as follows:
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of receiving Confidential Information from the City of Winnipeg and other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, ___________________________(insert name of legal name of counterparty) (the “Recipient”) agrees as follows:
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidant' is replaced with 'Recipient' in the introduction but is not changed throughout the document. This will cause confusion as to whether these terms refer to the same entity. Specifically in section 1 where 'Confidant' is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions : In this Agreement the following terms shall have the following meanings: a) “Agreement” means this agreement. b) “Confidant” means the Person named as such above. c) “Confidential Information” means any and all inform ation, regardless of form, format or medium (including without limitation visual or oral informatio n), of, related to, concerning, or resulting from, the City, the RFP and/or the Permitted Use, which co mes into the possession or knowledge of the Confidant, including, without limitation, the RFP, do cuments, business information, know how, data, trade secrets, processes, designs, communication s, materials, drawings, diagrams, computer programs, concepts, and any and all copies, reprodu ctions, modifications, and derivative works. d) “Effective Date” is the day and date last below written. e) “City” means The City of Winnipeg. f) “Permitted Use” means private evaluation by t he Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to t he RFP, and for no other purpose whatsoever. g) “Person” shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corp oration, partnership, other entity, or individual. h) “Third Party” means any Pers on other than City or Confidant.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidant' with 'Recipient' in the introductory clause. However, the 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Confidant'. This directly contradicts the definitions section, as the document now uses the term 'Confidant' without a clear, consistent reference in the introductory clause. This creates a misaligned terminology issue leading to confusion and a strong in-text contradiction, falling under the definition provided for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: “Confidential Information” means any and all inform ation, regardless of form, format or medium (including without limitation visual or oral informatio n), of, related to, concerning, or resulting from, the City, the RFP and/or the Permitted Use, which co mes into the possession or knowledge of the Confidant, including, without limitation, the RFP, do cuments, business information, know how, data, trade secrets, processes, designs, communication s, materials, drawings, diagrams, computer programs, concepts, and any and all copies, reprodu ctions, modifications, and derivative works.
perturbation.changed_text: “Confidential Information” means any and all data, regardless of form, format or medium (including without limitation visual or oral data), of, related to, concerning, or resulting from, the City, the RFP and/or the Permitted Use, which co mes into the possession or knowledge of the Confidant, including, without limitation, the RFP, do cuments, business information, know how, trade secrets, processes, designs, communication s, materials, drawings, diagrams, computer programs, concepts, and any and all copies, reprodu ctions, modifications, and derivative works.
perturbation.explanation: Changed 'information' to 'data' in the definition of 'Confidential Information'. This introduces an ambiguity: is 'Confidential Information' limited to 'data' or does it include other forms of information not explicitly mentioned such as oral communication? This contradicts further use of 'Confidential Information' where 'information' is implicitly expected to be included, such as Section 3a.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions : Confidant agrees that: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party; b) Confidant shall restrict access to Confidential Information only to its employees with a need to know to carry out the Permitted Use, and prior to disclosing same, each such employee shall be made aware of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and c) Confidant shall cause all of its applicable empl oyees to observe the terms of this Agreement, and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or any such employee.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' in the definition of 'Confidential Information' creates a strong in-text contradiction. The original text includes 'any and all information, regardless of form, format or medium'. The changed text narrows this to 'any and all data'. The contradicted text in Restrictions Section refers to 'Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence', implying that the definition of Confidential Information is all encompassing. By limiting Confidential Information to only 'data', it excludes other forms of information that would otherwise be considered confidential, such as oral communication, thereby contradicting the expectation in the Restrictions section that all confidential information is protected.

perturbation.original_text: “Permitted Use” means private evaluation by t he Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to t he RFP, and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: “Permitted Use” means internal review by the Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to the RFP, and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'private evaluation' to 'internal review' creates ambiguity about the scope of usage. Does 'internal review' allow for external consultants? The original context assumed an evaluation remains private, and 'internal review' doesn't ensure that, leading to conflict with section 3a where strictest confidence is required.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions : Confidant agrees that: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party; b) Confidant shall restrict access to Confidential Information only to its employees with a need to know to carry out the Permitted Use, and prior to disclosing same, each such employee shall be made aware of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and c) Confidant shall cause all of its applicable empl oyees to observe the terms of this Agreement, and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or any such employee.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'private evaluation' to 'internal review' broadens the scope of permitted use, potentially allowing external consultants to access confidential information. This directly contradicts section 3a, which mandates 'strictest confidence' and prohibits disclosure to any Third Party. While 'private evaluation' strongly implies no third-party involvement, 'internal review' is more ambiguous and could reasonably be interpreted to include external parties. Therefore, the change introduces a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Confidant acknowledges and agrees that Ci ty reserves the full independent right to modify the scope and content of Confidential Information ava ilable for access and/or use hereunder at any time and without prior notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges and agrees that Ci ty reserves the right to modify the scope and content of Confidential Information ava ilable for access and/or use hereunder at any time and without prior notice.
perturbation.explanation: Removing 'full independent' from the sentence changes the implication that the City has complete autonomy regarding the right. This clashes with clauses that dictate remedies to the City in case of a breach by the Confidant of contract terms. Section 5 notes about the City been entitle to interlocutory and permanent injunction relief to restrain any anticipated, present or continuing breach of this Agreement, however if it isn't independent it can create uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Continuing Obligations and Remedies : The obligations of Confidant under this Agreement shall not terminate but shall continue without limitation of time. Confidant acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any term or condition of this Agreement shall cause irreparable harm to City which cannot be adequately compensated for in damages, and acco rdingly Confidant agrees that City shall be entitled, in addition to any other remedies available to it, to interlocutory and permanent injunction relief to restrain any anticipated, present or continuing breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the City 'full independent right' to modify Confidential Information. The changed text removes 'full independent,' weakening the City's autonomy. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which entitles the City to injunction relief in case of a breach. The absence of 'full independent' introduces ambiguity regarding the City's entitlement, causing an in-text contradiction, as if it doesn't have that right, the remedy may not be possible.

perturbation.original_text: Confidant shall immediately on notice at any time from City return to City, or destroy, any and all Confidential In formation in accordance with City’s direction.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidant shall immediately on notice at any time from City return to City, or properly dispose of, any and all Confidential In formation in accordance with City’s direction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'destroy' to 'properly dispose of' introduces ambiguity. 'Properly dispose of' could be interpreted in many ways, including selling the information to a third party after anonymization. This directly contradicts section 3(a), which requires that Confidential Information be kept in the strictest confidence and not be disclosed to any Third Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Restrictions : Confidant agrees that: a) Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party; b) Confidant shall restrict access to Confidential Information only to its employees with a need to know to carry out the Permitted Use, and prior to disclosing same, each such employee shall be made aware of the terms and conditions of this Agreement; and c) Confidant shall cause all of its applicable empl oyees to observe the terms of this Agreement, and shall be responsible for any breach of the terms of this Agreement by it or any such employee.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'destroy' to 'properly dispose of' introduces ambiguity that directly contradicts the requirement in the 'Contradicted Text' section that 'Confidential Information shall be kept in the strictest confidence without limitation of time, and shall not be disclosed to any Third Party'. 'Properly dispose of' allows for interpretations that could involve disclosing information to a third party, creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: Confidant acknowledges and agrees that all rights in and to Confidential Information are and shall remain the sole property of City, and Confidant agrees that it shall not contest or challenge any of City’s rights in or to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Consultant acknowledges and agrees that all rights in and to Confidential Information are and shall remain the sole property of City, and Consultant agrees that it shall not contest or challenge any of City’s rights in or to any Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Confidant' is replaced with 'Consultant' in section 6 but is not changed throughout the document. This will cause confusion as to whether these terms refer to the same entity. This contradicts the definition in section 1, and also throughout the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions : In this Agreement the following terms shall have the following meanings: a) “Agreement” means this agreement. b) “Confidant” means the Person named as such above. c) “Confidential Information” means any and all inform ation, regardless of form, format or medium (including without limitation visual or oral informatio n), of, related to, concerning, or resulting from, the City, the RFP and/or the Permitted Use, which co mes into the possession or knowledge of the Confidant, including, without limitation, the RFP, do cuments, business information, know how, data, trade secrets, processes, designs, communication s, materials, drawings, diagrams, computer programs, concepts, and any and all copies, reprodu ctions, modifications, and derivative works. d) “Effective Date” is the day and date last below written. e) “City” means The City of Winnipeg. f) “Permitted Use” means private evaluation by t he Confidant solely for the purpose of preparing a submission(s) to City in response to t he RFP, and for no other purpose whatsoever. g) “Person” shall be broadly interpreted to include, without limitation, any corp oration, partnership, other entity, or individual. h) “Third Party” means any Pers on other than City or Confidant.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text refer to "Confidant", which has a specific definition. By changing "Confidant" to "Consultant" in the changed text, but not updating the definition or other instances of "Confidant" in the document, it introduces confusion as to which entity is being referred to in section 6. This directly contradicts the definition in section 1, creating a clear in-text contradiction according to the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
